# QC Monitor — Backtest Report (Phases 1–3)

*Generated by `scripts/backtest/{reconstruct_history,detect_crashes,signal_timing}.py`.
Regenerate by re-running those three scripts. Data: `backtest/data/`.*

This tests whether the monitor's indicators would have flagged the major market
crashes of the last ~7 years at a useful time. **Reminder:** the tool is a
*buy-the-panic timer*, not a crash predictor — so the question is whether each
indicator went red **near the bottom**, and how noisy it is the rest of the time.

---

## Data foundation (Phase 1)

- **1,860 trading days reconstructed**, 2019-01-02 → today, including COVID.
- Levels recomputed with the **exact production thresholds** (imported from
  `market_monitor.py`).
- Validated: matches the live CSV on overlapping days; `^TNX` range 0.50–4.99%
  (no scaling bug); spot-checks correct (VIX **82.69** on 2020-03-16; KRE red
  during the 2023 SVB week).

## Crash catalog (Phase 2)

| # | Event | Type | Peak → Trough | Depth | Recovery |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | COVID | broad bear | 2020-02-19 → 03-23 | **−33.9%** | 2020-08-18 |
| 2 | 2022 bear market | broad bear | 2022-01-03 → 2022-10-12 | **−25.4%** | 2024-01-19 |
| 3 | Regional-bank crisis (SVB) | sector (banks) | 2023-02-07 → 2023-05-04 | **−43.8%** | 2024-11-06 |
| 4 | Aug-2024 VIX spike | vol spike | 2024-07-16 → 2024-08-05 | **−8.5%** | 2024-09-19 |
| 5 | 2025 tariff selloff | broad correction | 2025-02-19 → 2025-04-08 | **−18.9%** | 2025-06-27 |

Broad crashes = S&P ≥10% drawdown; sector crises = rolling 21-day return ≤ −15%
that the broad market shrugged off; Aug-2024 curated (too brief for the rules).

## Signal timing (Phase 3)

Cell = days the **first RED** signal fired **before the bottom** (＋ = early,
− = late, `a` = amber only, `.` = silent). Window = decline + 30 days.

| event | VIX | KRE | QQQ | NVDA | Brent | US10Y |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| COVID | +25 | +20 | +11 | +7 | . | . |
| 2022 bear | +260 | a | +212 | +219 | a | . |
| 2023 banks | a | +56 | +72 | a | . | . |
| Aug-24 VIX | +0 | . | a | a | . | . |
| 2025 tariff | +5 | −3 | +4 | +4 | . | a |

**Hit rate across the 5 crashes:**

| Indicator | Red | Amber+ | Median red lead vs trough |
|---|---|---|---|
| VIX | 4/5 | 5/5 | +15d |
| KRE | 3/5 | 4/5 | +20d |
| QQQ | 4/5 | 5/5 | +41d |
| NVDA | 3/5 | 5/5 | +7d |
| Brent (spike) | 0/5 | 1/5 | — |
| US10Y | 0/5 | 1/5 | — |

**False-alarm check** — RED days inside vs outside the 5 crash windows:

| Indicator | In-crash | Outside | % of red days real |
|---|---|---|---|
| VIX | 100 | 49 | 67% |
| KRE | 37 | 3 | **92%** |
| QQQ | 155 | 52 | 75% |
| NVDA | 154 | 224 | **41%** |
| Brent | 0 | 0 | n/a (never red) |
| US10Y | 0 | 0 | n/a (never red) |

Overall (production aggregate, incl. `brent_low`): non-green **87% of all days** —
398 in-crash vs 1,222 outside.

---

## Key findings

1. **The sharp-panic signals work.** VIX + QQQ flagged every fast crash near the
   bottom — COVID, Aug-2024, and 2025 within days of the low. **KRE nailed its
   designed scenario**: red in March 2023, 56 days before the bank-crisis bottom,
   with the cleanest record of all (92% of its red days were real crises).

2. **Slow bears fire far too early.** In the 2022 grind, VIX/QQQ/NVDA went red
   200–260 days before the bottom. They correctly flagged the *regime*, but a red
   light 8 months before the low is not a buy signal.

3. **Two thresholds are effectively dead.** **Brent > $130** and **US10Y > 5.5%**
   never triggered once in 7 years (10Y peaked ~5%, Brent ~$128). Their red bands
   are set beyond what actually happens.

4. **NVDA is the cry-wolf signal.** −35% fired red on **224 days outside any
   crash** — only 41% of its red days were real. It is the noisiest indicator.

5. **The overall signal is dominated by noise** (non-green 87% of days), largely
   from `brent_low` (oil under $80 is normal). The aggregate, as wired, is not a
   useful alert.

These set up Phase 5 calibration: lower/retire the dead Brent & US10Y red bands,
tame NVDA, pull `brent_low` out of the buy-signal aggregate, and treat VIX/KRE/QQQ
as the workhorses (ideally edge-triggered to stop slow-bear over-firing).

## Caveats

- **5 events ≠ statistics.** This is calibration and sanity-checking, not proof.
- **Hurricane** indicator excluded (not backtestable from current sources).
- **`brent_low`** excluded from the timing analysis (deescalation signal, not a
  crash marker).
- Forward-return payoff (does buying the signal pay?) is **Phase 4**, not yet run.
